title: SAP Clean Core Ties Decoupled Extensions Directly to Lower TCO
intent: Ground side-by-side modernization and AMS cost reduction in SAP’s own clean-core position.

source.title: Discover How to Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud the Right Way
source.methodology: SAP feature article on clean core and extensibility.

fact.primary_stat: SAP said on August 12, 2025 that clean core helps organizations minimize technical debt, simplify maintenance and upgrades, and lower total cost of ownership.
supporting_stats: SAP stated that ERP landscapes weighed down by custom code and hard-to-maintain integrations limit flexibility and increase total cost of ownership.
SAP described side-by-side extensibility as scalable, decoupled applications that run independently from the core.
SAP said extension compliance levels should be assessed based on decoupling, upgrade safety, and maintainability.

business_relevance: Cost reduction in SAP landscapes is explicitly linked to decoupling and extension discipline.
The architecture conversation is no longer just technical purity; it is a maintenance economics issue.
dzmitryi_commentary: My angle here is pragmatic: you do not need to keep piling logic into the ERP or into an expensive platform dependency to modernize AMS. The business value comes from designing the right side-by-side capabilities around the core: triage, retrieval, automation, diagnostics, and process memory, all with clean contracts and measurable support savings.

provenance.note: Extracted and curated by Dzmitryi Kharlanau; enriched for attribution and crawler indexing.

meta.summary: Ground side-by-side modernization and AMS cost reduction in SAP’s own clean-core position.
